author: doll-agent
content:
Closed as not actionable as standalone work. Audit details: of the ~500-line files for chebyshev/legendre/laguerre/hermite/hermite_e, only ~50 lines per file is truly identical across bases (struct field declarations, with_domain/with_window setters, coeffs/degree accessors). The remaining ~450 lines per file is genuinely basis-specific: recurrence relations (T_n+1 = 2x*T_n - T_n-1 vs L_n+1 = ((2n+1-x)*L_n - n*L_n-1)/(n+1) etc.), Vandermonde matrix construction, Gauss quadrature points and weights, basis-to-power-basis conversion, multiplication via three-term recurrence, etc. — all mathematically distinct.

The natural place for a deeper unification is alongside #612 (lift Poly trait from f64 to T: Element + Float and Complex), where the trait redesign would absorb the field declarations and accessors into a generic shape. Trying to extract a macro_rules! shell now would risk regressions in well-tested numerical kernels for ~5% line savings.

Closing as superseded-by-context: #612 is the right milestone for any code-organization refactor of this surface.
